title: Local obituary: Madison Tesniere, 20-year-old from Raymond, N.H.
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Madison Danielle Tesniere, 20, from Raymond, N.H., passed away unexpectedly on May 23, 2026, in Lawrence, Massachusetts.
Maddi grew up in Fremont and Raymond, N.H., excelling in athletics and earning a certification as a Med Tech.
Calling hours are on May 29, 2026, at Brewitt Funeral Home, with a graveside service at New Pine Grove Cemetery.

title: Registration to open for World Cup Fan Fest after city unveils 6 watch parties
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Registration for the Boston Fan Festival at City Hall Plaza opens Thursday at 2 p.m. via the Boston 26 website.
The free Fan Festival will run from June 12 to June 27 and accommodate about 5,000 fans daily.
Boston will host six World Cup watch parties in various neighborhoods; three locations are confirmed so far.

title: Here’s what Boston meteorologists are saying about possible showers into the weekend
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Boston meteorologists predict cooler temperatures and scattered showers in southern New England through Thursday afternoon.
National Weather Service forecasts the bulk of the rain to arrive Friday night, with windy conditions Saturday.
Cindy Fitzgibbon of WCVB warns of a chilly rain and wind on Saturday, with inland temperatures around 70°F.

title: State trooper hospitalized after gun accidentally discharges in barn, officials say
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A Massachusetts state trooper was injured after his gun accidentally discharged in the Mounted Unit barn in Acton.
The trooper was treated for non-life-threatening injuries at Lahey Hospital and released to recover at home.
The incident is under investigation, and the trooper is on administrative leave per policy.

title: Ex-CIA official charged with stealing over $40 million in gold bars from the federal government
author: The Associated Press
bullets: Ex-CIA official David Rush charged with stealing over $40 million in gold bars from federal government.
FBI found more than 300 gold bars, $2 million cash, and 35 luxury watches seized from Rush's home.
Rush allegedly lied about education, military background, and his lawyer declined to comment on the charges.

title: Rapoport: Patriots, Eagles ‘not particularly close’ on a potential A.J. Brown trade
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots and Eagles are not close to finalizing a trade for A.J. Brown, according to Ian Rapoport.
The Eagles seek a 2027 first-round pick for Brown, but the Patriots are currently unwilling to meet this demand.
Any trade discussions are likely to progress after June 1 when Brown's cap hit for the Eagles would decrease.

title: Is Simon Nemec a player the Bruins should target this offseason?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins are considering acquiring Devils defenseman Simon Nemec for his offensive skillset and potential development.
Nemec has struggled with consistency in three NHL seasons, appearing in 155 games with career-highs in 11 goals and 26 points in 2025-26.
New Jersey Devils may trade Nemec due to his pending restricted free agent status and past inconsistent performances.

title: Waltham animal control officers recover ‘pile ’o pigs’ dumped in woods, cemetery
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: 19 guinea pigs were found abandoned in a Waltham cemetery and woods by animal control officers.
Waltham Animal Control cites stricter regulations and reduced demand as reasons breeders are abandoning guinea pigs.
Community resources like The Guinea Pig Sanctuary have been key in rescuing and rehoming the animals.

title: What Drake Maye, Milton Williams said of A.J. Brown’s potential arrival in New England
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: A.J. Brown, currently with the Eagles, is linked to potential summer trade to the New England Patriots.
Trading Brown after June 1 reduces Eagles' dead cap hit from $43.3 million to $16.3 million.
Patriots players, including Drake Maye and Milton Williams, express optimism about Brown's potential arrival.

title: Something fishy? Large groups of dead fish spotted in Boston, Worcester ponds
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Dead fish observed in Boston and Worcester are part of a natural seasonal event, not a public safety concern.
Fish kills in Leverett Pond and Charles River linked to low oxygen levels and high temperatures, says MassWildlife.
Nearly 1,000 fish died at University Park in Worcester due to similar environmental conditions, reported the Telegram & Gazette.
